normalization_rules.reason: Store lists physical systems, not mixes/sends.
Store lists the system, not its send/output.
Split vague IFB interface into real pieces.
Avoid generic 'speaker processor' where specific processor type is known.

normalization_rules.old: Tie Line / Input / Output / Send / Return / Bus / CH

normalization_rules.canonical: TT patch cable + source endpoint in checklist
normalization_rules.reason: Aux output is a jack/route point, not equipment.

normalization_rules.canonical: Console with stereo FX return + checklist endpoint
normalization_rules.reason: FX return is a destination endpoint.

levels.brief: Patch a stereo pair into line inputs. Keep left and right channels consistent.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
levels.notes: Preserves user's approved logic; spelling cleaned. Tie-line/endpoint terms kept out of equipment.

levels.brief: Send multiple channels to an effect to create a shared ambience.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
checklist: Aux output is a jack/connection point, not purchasable gear.
Patchbay is equipment; patch points are checklist concepts.
levels.notes: Preferred: Reverb unit. Acceptable: Plate reverb or Delay/reverb unit. Route endpoints separated from purchasable gear.

levels.brief: Patch a drum kit to mic preamps.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
checklist: Close mics land on assigned mic preamps.
Stereo pair must include both sides.
Console/preamps are gear; individual inputs are route endpoints.
Need enough mics for the kit.
levels.notes: Preserves user's approved equipment logic, with spelling cleaned.

levels.brief: Patch a stereo-miked piano to mic preamps. Send the feed to the player's headphones.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
checklist: Tie line/input labels stay in checklist, not store.
Stereo pair must include both sides.
Aux output is a jack/connection point; TT cable is equipment.
levels.notes: Converted tie lines, console inputs, aux output, and headphone amp input into connection checklist; equipment list now contains physical gear.

levels.brief: Send signal to a recorder and a live broadcast feed.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
checklist: Host tie line/input names stay in checklist.
Program bus output is an endpoint.
Balanced TRS cable pair / USB cable
levels.notes: Converted program bus and encoder inputs to checklist endpoints; required equipment now represents physical broadcast gear.

levels.brief: Route program-minus-talent and talkback through the IFB.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
levels.notes: Endpoints are now connection requirements; store contains IFB and talkback gear.

levels.brief: Send IFB to the reporter. Patch reporter return to field recorder.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
levels.notes: Field reporter return and IFB send are endpoints, not store gear.

levels.brief: Route host microphone to the console and feed program to the stream.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
checklist: Host tie line/input names stay in checklist.
Program output and encoder input are endpoints.
levels.notes: Host tie line, program bus output, and stream encoder input now appear as connection points only.

levels.brief: Route mic to preamp to DAW for Foley.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
checklist: Cue output is a route endpoint.
levels.notes: ADR booth tie line, preamp I/O, interface input, cue output, and headphone amp are checklist endpoints.

levels.brief: Route the print through the loudness meter, into the print recorder.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
checklist: Stereo pair must include both sides.
levels.notes: DAW print outputs, meter I/O, and recorder inputs are connection points; equipment is meter, recorder, interface/I/O, and cable loom.

levels.brief: Feed the print into the downmix encoder and route the downmix to QC.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
checklist: Balanced TRS cable pair / DB25 loom
levels.notes: Downmix encoder I/O and QC recorder I/O are endpoints; gear is encoder, recorder, interface, and cabling.

levels.brief: Route the ADR booth through preamp and send cue mix back to the booth.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
checklist: Cue output/headphone amp input are endpoints.
levels.notes: ADR tie line, preamp I/O, interface input, cue output, and booth headphone amp are route endpoints.

levels.brief: Record multiple weapons at multiple distances.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
levels.notes: Build-a-Room stays physical/session setup; middleware objects are out of scope for this board type.

levels.brief: Set up a session to record a group of 12 background voice actors.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
levels.notes: Corrected spelling and shifted from game middleware objects to physical loop-group recording setup.

levels.brief: Record Foley for an immersive audio release.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
levels.notes: Corrected spelling and changed software event objects to capture-room equipment.

levels.brief: Record vehicles for an immersive audio release.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
levels.notes: Changed from unrelated middleware event labels to field-recording gear.

levels.brief: Patch mix to front-fills and mains.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
checklist: Lead source/stagebox input are connection points.
Stereo pair must include both sides.
levels.notes: Lead source, stagebox input, main outputs, and processor inputs are checklist endpoints. Gear is mic, stagebox, console, processors, speakers/amps, and cables.

levels.brief: Patch stereo aux to an in-ear monitor.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
checklist: Stereo pair must include both sides.
levels.notes: FOH aux outputs and IEM transmitter inputs are connection points. Gear is console, IEM transmitter/receiver, earbuds, and cabling.

levels.brief: Patch mix to a recorder and live stream.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
checklist: Broadcast split outputs are connection points.
Stereo pair must include both sides.
levels.notes: Broadcast split outputs and record outputs are connection points; equipment is console/splitter/recorder/encoder/cables.

levels.brief: Patch a handheld dynamic mic into the stagebox and feed mains to the PA system.
levels.instruction: Choose the equipment needed for this job.
checklist: Matrix router / system processor left input
Matrix router / system processor right input
Stereo pair must include both sides.
levels.notes: Stagebox and main output endpoints stay in checklist; equipment list uses mic, console, stagebox, matrix router, PA, and cables.
